mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates compress equity discount rates, directly lifting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding half a percentage point signals labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm synchronized consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive SPX direction with accuracy > 55% over 100 observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum yields negative SPX 5-day returns in > 65% of cases during late-cycle expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with persistently low federal funds rates compresses real yields and signals impending monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS predicts positive SPX returns in > 60% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates sustained labor market expansion, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS momentum remains below 0.02 across all 3-year rolling windows, or positive payroll growth correlates with negative SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real gross domestic product confirms an economic recession, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 70% of quarters where GDPC1 shows negative QoQ growth.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and rising unemployment signals a broad-based supply-side contraction, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal of falling INDPRO and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative SPX direction with statistical significance (p > 0.05) over 50 observations.
